name: Extension Guide
description: Step-by-step instructions for extending the system with new query operations, mutations, validation rules, auth middleware, rate limiters, error types, and storage operations.

intent.what: Provides comprehensive guidance for adding new functionality to the system while maintaining architecture consistency, schema-agnostic design, and production-grade patterns.
intent.why: Enables developers and AI agents to extend the system correctly without breaking existing patterns, introducing domain logic, or compromising resilience features.

inputs.name: extensionType
inputs.description: Type of extension (query, mutation, validation, auth, rate-limit, error, storage)

inputs.name: implementation
inputs.description: Implementation details specific to the extension type

outputs.description: Extended system functionality following established patterns

steps: Identify the extension type and target module
Follow the established pattern for that extension type
Export from the appropriate index file
Update skill files if adding new public APIs
Test the extension with various inputs and edge cases

logic.summary: Extensions follow established architectural patterns to maintain consistency. New queries use the query builder pattern with retry and caching support. New mutations follow validate-execute-invalidate pattern. Auth middleware uses enforceAuth/withAuth patterns. Rate limiters use the sliding window implementation. All extensions must remain schema-agnostic and domain-independent.

flow.detail: Determine which module the extension belongs to (database, auth, storage, utils)
Use existing patterns for query building, mutation execution, or middleware
Add export to module index and re-export from src/lib/index.ts
Update skill files if adding new public APIs
Test with various inputs, edge cases, and error conditions

rules: All extensions must remain schema-agnostic (no hardcoded tables/columns)
New query operations must support caching, retry, and timeout
New mutations must invalidate cache for affected tables
Validation must use Zod schemas with validateInput/validateOrFail
Error types must extend SupabaseError base class
Rate limiters must use the sliding window implementation
anti_patterns: Do not add domain-specific logic to core modules
Do not bypass caching or retry logic without justification
Do not create duplicate functionality that exists elsewhere
Do hardcode table names, column names, or business rules
Do not skip error normalization in new operations
Do not forget to export new functions from index files

extension_patterns.name: Query Operation
extension_patterns.pattern: Build query → Apply filters → Execute with retry → Cache result

extension_patterns.name: Mutation Operation
extension_patterns.pattern: Validate input → Execute mutation → Invalidate cache → Return result

extension_patterns.name: Auth Middleware
extension_patterns.pattern: Get session → Verify claims → Inject context → Execute handler

extension_patterns.name: Validation Schema
extension_patterns.pattern: Define Zod schema → Validate data → Return errors or throw

extension_patterns.name: Rate Limiter
extension_patterns.pattern: Check counter → Allow/deny → Update counter → Cleanup expired

extension_patterns.name: Error Type
extension_patterns.pattern: Extend SupabaseError → Set code → Add custom properties

extension_patterns.name: Storage Operation
extension_patterns.pattern: Validate file → Execute operation → Handle retry → Return result

when_to_use: When adding new database query or mutation operations
When defining Zod schemas for input validation
When adding authentication middleware to endpoints
When implementing rate limiting for new endpoints
When creating custom error types for specific scenarios
when_not_to_use: When modifying existing functionality (follow change management process)
When adding domain-specific business logic (implement in separate layer)
When bypassing established patterns without architectural review
reasoning_steps: Determine the appropriate module for the extension
Review existing patterns in that module
Add proper error handling, retry, and caching where applicable
Export from index files and update documentation
Test with various inputs and edge cases
